Compute external XtY predictions and aggregate multiple external models

calcExtXtY.Rd

Compute external-model XtY predictions in the summary-statistics setting and, when more than one external model is supplied, aggregate them into a single combined prediction. Aggregation is controlled by multi.method: "ind" keeps each model independent, "PCA" aggregates via the first principal component of the normalised external coefficients, and "stacking" learns optimal stacking weights by minimising the summary-statistic least-squares criterion using XtX and XtY.

Usage

calcExtXtY(XtX, XtY, beta.external, multi.method)

Arguments

XtX

A p x p sparse LD reference matrix.

XtY

A numeric vector of marginal correlations (length p).

beta.external

A p x M matrix of external model coefficients (no intercept).

multi.method

A string: "ind", "PCA", or "stacking".

Value

A list with two elements:

beta.external

The external coefficient matrix. For "PCA" and "stacking", this is a p x 1 aggregated coefficient vector; for "ind", this is the original input.

XtY.external

A p x M' matrix of external XtY predictions, where M' = 1 for "PCA" and "stacking" and M' = M for "ind".

Details

Used internally by BRIERs, but exposed for users who want to compute external XtY predictions independently of the BRIER fitting framework.

See also

BRIERs, calLD, calcExtY

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# XtX: sparse p x p LD matrix
# XtY: length-p marginal correlation vector
# beta.external: p x M external coefficient matrix

ext <- calcExtXtY(XtX, XtY, beta.external, multi.method = "ind")
dim(ext$XtY.external)
} # }
